selected_protocol.title: Cryopreservation of mammalian cells using controlled-rate freezing
selected_protocol.description: Example normalized protocol candidate showing the kind of search result that can be selected for grounding.

candidates.title: Cryopreservation of mammalian cells using controlled-rate freezing
candidates.description: Example normalized protocol candidate showing the kind of search result that can be selected for grounding.

candidates.title: Post-thaw viability workflow for adherent mammalian cells
candidates.description: Second candidate illustrating the list of nearby protocols available to the selector.

steps.title: Expand cells to target confluence
steps.description: Grow HeLa cells under standard culture conditions until they reach 70 to 80 percent confluence before harvest.

steps.title: Prepare freezing suspension
steps.description: Harvest cells, count viable cells, and resuspend at the target density in cryopreservation medium.

steps.title: Freeze using controlled-rate cooling
steps.description: Aliquot cells into cryovials and cool at approximately minus 1 degree C per minute before long-term storage.

steps.title: Thaw and wash cells
steps.description: Rapidly thaw cryovials in a 37 C water bath and dilute cells into pre-warmed medium before centrifugation.

gaps: Exact intracellular trehalose loading method is not specified in the source protocol.
Secondary recovery assay reagents would need downstream enrichment if not listed here.
